name: SREDNOFF OS (Claude Code)

notes.skills-library-metadata-validator: v1.17: validate-skills-library.ps1/.sh - fast name/description/frontmatter smoke check for templates/claude-md-os/skills-library/*/SKILL.md, ported concept from srednoff-os's quick-validate-all-skills.ps1 'fast' mode (their 'full' mode shells out to an external Codex-specific validator we don't have and don't need). Wired into doctor.ps1/.sh (new 'skills-library' check) and CI (registry-and-evals job + windows-powershell job).
notes.skills-library-303-installable-skills:
v1.17, Stage 3 of the Codex-merge plan. Discovery that changed the whole stage's scope: CORE-300.md already had metadata stubs (name+tags+source, empty description) for 306/308 of srednoff-os's (Codex sibling) skills, from a v1.5-era bulk catalog expansion - so Stage 3 was NOT a fresh 308-skill import, it was: backfill descriptions for the existing stubs, build actual installable SKILL.md content in a new templates/claude-md-os/skills-library/ (adapted: Codex->Claude Code, .codex/->.claude/), and wire gen-profile-lock to install a capped, tag-matched subset. Final accounting of the 308: 301 stubs got description backfill + full skill-content import; 2 were genuinely missing and added as new CORE-300.md entries (external-prompt-pattern-miner, promptfoo-evals-redteam-ci) with full import; 2 are the donor's own internal meta-concepts already superseded by our own work (quality-cost-skill-kernel - our CORE-300.md itself is the curated catalog; source-ranking-roi-selector - already ported as our source-ranker.ps1/.sh in v1.16) - description backfilled with an explanatory note, no content imported; 3 are name collisions with our own existing canonical skills (github-research, production-review, seo) - left completely untouched, no changes, no import, to avoid true catalog duplicates. registry-audit stayed at 0 duplicates throughout (2029 total records, +2 net from the 2 new entries). gen-profile-lock.ps1/.sh install up to 20 tag-matched skills from skills-library into a project's .claude/skills/ when generating PROFILE.lock (~100 tokens/skill at session start makes installing all 303 unconditionally a real context-budget mistake, not a style choice). Two real bugs found and fixed during implementation: (1) the skill-install matching was initially written against the general candidate list ($cand, capped at 40 entries) rather than skills-library/index.json directly - a high-frequency tag like 'web' saturates that cap before a less-common tag like '3d' ever gets a slot, silently starving skill installation for exactly the projects most likely to want it (found by testing a react-three-fiber package.json: 0 skills installed despite correct 3d/animation tags); fixed by matching independently against skills-library/index.json. (2) Python's Path.write_text() on Windows silently translates 
 to 
 unless newline='' is passed - CORE-300.md, CORE-300.json, skills-library/index.json, and all 303 SKILL.md files were written with CRLF by the one-off import script, which broke the bash (but not PowerShell) metadata validator in a very confusing way (regex logic that worked standalone failed identically for all 303 files when run in the real script) before being traced to a literal  appended to every line-comparison value; fixed the import script (newline='
' on every write_text call, for future re-runs) and stripped CR from all four already-written artifact types, then regenerated CORE-300.json from the now-clean CORE-300.md via the canonical generator rather than hand-patching it further.
notes.init-home-directory-safety-guard: v1.16, CRITICAL FIX found during Stage 2 testing: init-claude-project.sh (bash only - the PowerShell port never had this logic) had an unbounded 'prefer the git root' walk (git rev-parse --show-toplevel) that could escape past the intended project into ANY unrelated ancestor .git, including a personal repo at $HOME. A real incident during testing: a scratch path nested under $HOME/AppData/... resolved to $HOME because $HOME/.git existed (0 commits, unrelated personal repo), silently deploying the full OS - rules/hooks/skills/CLAUDE.md/etc. - directly into the user's home directory. Verified via .bak-file absence and file-hash comparison that nothing pre-existing was overwritten; cleaned up with explicit user permission. Fixed by (1) removing the git-root walk entirely from init-claude-project.sh, bringing it into parity with the .ps1 port (which never had this logic and was never affected), and (2) adding a hard $HOME/$env:USERPROFILE guard to BOTH scripts as defense in depth, regardless of how a bad target could be reached in the future. Regression-tested against the exact original failure scenario.
notes.design-source-registry: v1.16, ported from srednoff-os (Codex sibling): registry/design-source-registry.json, 17 external UI/3D/design/growth sources (shadcn, 21st.dev, Three.js, react-three-fiber, Sketchfab, etc.) with license/provenance/vetted/copy_policy/requires_user_prompt metadata. Dropped the donor's hardcoded 'version' field (this repo's registries don't carry a per-file version string, to avoid drift from version.json). validate-source-registry.ps1/.sh (bash port written from scratch - donor never shipped one) check required fields, duplicate ids, risk enum, and that high-risk sources require a user prompt.
notes.source-ranker: v1.16, ported and adapted from srednoff-os's srednoff-os-source-ranker.ps1 (concept and scoring logic only, rewritten for this repo's registry layout): scores design-source-registry.json entries against a task brief using domain match, named-source detection, risk penalty, vetted/license/provenance bonuses and gates, mode-router integration, and optional mcp-inventory connector-enabled bonus. source-ranker.sh is a from-scratch bash 3.2 port (none existed upstream) - verified score-for-score identical output against the PowerShell version on multiple test briefs (e.g. both rank shadcn/ui registry at 33 and Three.js-family sources at 14.25 for matching briefs). One documented simplification: the bash port omits per-match 'reasons' tracking (debug/informational only, does not affect score or gates) to avoid a much heavier jq implementation for no functional gain.
notes.catalog-json-export: v1.16: registry/CORE-300.json, a generated machine-readable export of CORE-300.md for external tools (skill installers, selectors, dashboards) - same relationship to CORE-300.md as PROFILE.lock has to a project. Originated as an external contribution via a real GitHub PR (elysosss/srednoff-os-for-claude#1, generated with Claude Code) - reviewed by actually running both gen-catalog-json.sh/.ps1 against the real 2027-record registry rather than trusting the PR's claimed numbers (confirmed: 2027/2027, G1=393/G2=1490/G3=140, the 5 records with no source code). Found and fixed during review: the .ps1 port (submitted untested - 'no pwsh here, please verify on Windows') failed to parse at all on real PowerShell 5.1 due to a literal em-dash in the source, the exact non-ASCII-in-.ps1 bug class this repo already had a house rule against (see v1.12); fixed by building the separator via [char]0x2014. Also found 4 additional records (not caught by the original PR) that get group:null due to an 'Анти-overlap' subheader resetting the group tracker in CORE-300.md's structure - documented, not fixed (same principle as the 5 source-null records: don't restructure the source of truth as a side effect of reviewing someone else's PR). --check wired into doctor.ps1/.sh (new 'catalog-json' check) and CI (registry-and-evals job) as a drift gate. See registry/RFC-CATALOG-JSON.md for full detail and provenance.
notes.sync-refresh-flag: v1.16: apply-os-all.ps1/.sh gained -Sync/--sync (default off, backward-compatible) to ALSO refresh already-initialized projects, not just apply to missing ones - re-runs init-claude-project on them, which already does hash-diff + timestamped backup + CLAUDE.md preservation safely. Implemented as a flag on the existing script rather than a new parallel sync script (like srednoff-os's sync-codex-skills-to-projects.ps1) because init-claude-project.ps1/.sh already does everything a sync needs; a separate script would have duplicated that logic. Tested end-to-end on an isolated fixture (stale rule file correctly refreshed with a timestamped backup) on both platforms.
notes.template-drift-check: v1.16: gen-profile-lock.ps1/.sh now stamp an 'OS version: vX.XX' line into PROFILE.lock.md (read from registry/version.json at generation time). doctor.ps1/.sh gained a new 'template-drift' check (WARN, never FAIL) comparing a project's stamped version against the current template version, pointing at apply-os-all -Sync when they differ. Concept from srednoff-os's Srednoff-OS-doctor drift awareness, implemented via a lightweight stamp rather than a separate marker file.
notes.quality-modes: v1.15, ported concept from srednoff-os (Codex sibling): mode-router now classifies a brief into 5 quality modes (fast/standard/production/critical/turbo, registry/quality-modes.json) instead of 3 (normal/deep/turbo). Each mode also reports legacy_mode (normal/deep/turbo) so any pre-v1.15 consumer keeps working. fast=lean/8 (typo/small-fix), standard=balanced/16 (default), production=deep/24 (launch/deploy/SEO/growth/mobile/3D), critical=deep/32 (security/auth/payments/DB-migration - bigger budget than production because the risk is higher), turbo=turbo/48 (literal TURBO only, unchanged). Deliberately did NOT port the donor's bare '\baudit\b' critical-trigger - it false-positived on "SEO audit"/"content audit" in our own quality-mode-fixtures.json; security/compliance triggers already cover the intended case.
notes.powershell-static-analysis-ci: v1.15: windows-powershell CI job gained a PowerShell parse-check (Parser::ParseFile over every .ps1) and PSScriptAnalyzer -Severity Error, closing the asymmetry where bash had shellcheck but PowerShell had no static analysis at all - ported from srednoff-os's CI approach.
notes.quality-release-evidence-docs: v1.15: added QUALITY.md and RELEASE.md at repo root (concept ported from srednoff-os), listing every verified check with the exact command that produces it, plus explicit 'What This Does Not Promise' and 'Known Residual Risks' sections - no number in either file is asserted without a re-runnable command.
notes.rules-readme-paths-scoping-doc: v1.15: added .claude/rules/README.md documenting the native Claude Code paths: frontmatter feature for scoping project-specific rules to a glob (confirmed via official docs research). The 10 shared core rules deliberately do NOT use paths: - they are cross-cutting and must stay always-active.
notes.session-start-banner-oneline: SessionStart banner (session-start-hook.ps1/.sh) now emits a single scannable summary line first - [SREDNOFF OS: ACTIVE] project='name' tags=... rules=N loaded PROFILE.lock=cached|not generated - followed by the fuller agent-facing detail paragraph on a second line. Rule count is computed by counting .claude/rules/*.md at runtime instead of being hardcoded, so it can't drift from the actual rule set. Motivation: research into Claude Desktop's Code tab found no statusLine/footer-badge equivalent there (Desktop shares CLAUDE.md/hooks/settings/skills/MCP with the CLI per its docs, but has its own fixed, non-scriptable ambient UI - diff-stat badge, usage ring, CI status bar - with no user-hookable persistent surface). The SessionStart banner is the only thing that reliably renders in both CLI and Desktop transcripts without the user asking, so it was tightened to be legible at a glance instead of inventing a Desktop badge the product doesn't expose.
notes.profile-lock-enforcement-gate: PreToolUse(Edit|Write|MultiEdit) hook (require-profile-lock-read) denies the first edit of a session in a project with .claude/PROFILE.lock.md until it's actually been read (verified by a companion PostToolUse(Read) marker hook, mark-profile-lock-read). Opt-in via settings.example.json like the security hooks. Built after a real case where an embedded CLAUDE.md banner alone did not cause compliance - see 70-skills-registry.md.
notes.model-routing-guidance: Advisory only for the main session (the agent follows rule 80 and can switch via /model); actionable for delegated sub-agents via the Agent tool's per-call `model` parameter. Nothing auto-switches the main session model - this is guidance, not an automated router.
notes.catalog-external-entries: The ~569 WSH/VOLT/FTB/GH catalog references are an unvetted DISCOVERY surface, not license-cleared endorsements. They stay unvetted until github-research per rule 70. INST/ANTH entries are installed/official and higher-confidence.
notes.bash-support: Scripts are bash-3.2-compatible (macOS /bin/bash 3.2.57). CI runs shellcheck to keep them that way.

ported_from: srednoff-os (Codex sibling, github.com/srednoff888-art/srednoff-os) - concepts only, rewritten for Claude Code/PowerShell
